channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rosslyn BMW plant reaches milestone with 100,000th fourth-generation X3
item.description: BMW Group’s Rosslyn plant, located just north of Pretoria, has achieved a significant milestone by producing its 100,000th fourth-generation BMW X3. This particular model, a Fire Red X3 plug-in hybrid...

item.title: China’s AI plan aims to boost jobs while managing long-term employment risks
item.description: China is banking on a massive, society-wide rollout of artificial intelligence to help create new jobs and support its slowing economy, even as global experts warn of major disruptions to work. Policy...

item.title: Landslides Block Karakoram Highway After Heavy Rain and Snowfall in GB
item.description: Gilgit-Baltistan is facing severe weather conditions as fresh rain and snowfall have triggered landslides at multiple locations along the Karakoram Highway (KKH). The Diamer and Kohistan districts are...

item.title: Historical Challenges of Achieving Regime Change Through Air Power
item.description: The article analyzes the limitations of relying on air power alone to achieve regime change, focusing on the recent U.S. and Israeli military actions against Iran. It argues that history demonstrates ...

item.title: First Bank Faces Backlash Over Staff Layoff Method
item.description: First Bank Nigeria don cause wahala online as dem latest layoff method don dey widely discussed. According to social media reports, di bank bring new non-core staff go some branches, make the old non-...

item.title: Bellarmine Mugabe to Apply for Bail in Johannesburg Shooting Case
item.description: Bellarmine Mugabe, son of the late former Zimbabwean president, along with his co-accused Tobias Matonhodze, is set to apply for bail at the Alexandra Magistrate's Court in Johannesburg. The two face ...

item.title: Dateline Resources Expands Music Valley Rare Earths Project in California
item.description: Dateline Resources has significantly expanded its Music Valley project in California by lodging 969 new claims, increasing the total footprint to 1,026 contiguous claims covering 20,520 acres—an 1800 ...

item.title: NDP MP Lori Idlout joins Liberals, affecting PM Carney's path to majority
item.description: Lori Idlout, a Member of Parliament representing the New Democratic Party (NDP), has officially crossed the floor to join the Liberal Party. This move reduces the NDP's caucus by one seat and brings t...

item.title: US Lawmakers Express Concerns Over Trump Administration’s Iran Military Strategy
item.description: After a secret congressional briefing, US lawmakers from both political parties have voiced serious worries about the Trump administration’s approach to the ongoing US-Israeli military campaign in Ira...

item.title: Brumbies make nine changes to starting XV for trip to Fiji
item.description: The ACT Brumbies have announced significant changes to their starting lineup ahead of their Super Rugby clash against the Fijian Drua at Ba's Govind Park. Coach Stephen Larkham has made nine alteratio...

item.title: Public Protector investigates irregular appointments and prolonged sick leave in Gauteng Legislature
item.description: The Public Protector has launched an investigation into the Gauteng Legislature over allegations of irregular appointments and misuse of public funds. Former Mpumalanga Legislature secretary Linda Mwa...

item.title: US Denies Escorting Oil Tankers Through Hormuz; 16 Iranian Minelaying Ships Destroyed Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The United States has clarified that it has not escorted any commercial oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, after an earlier social media claim by the US Secretary of Energy was deleted. The sta...

item.title: Live Updates: U.S. and Israel Launch Strikes on Iran Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: On February 28, 2026, the United States and Israel initiated coordinated airstrikes across Iran under Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion, targeting military sites, air defenses, missile facilities,...

item.title: ASEAN Economic Managers to Discuss Digital Economy, Cross-Border Payments, and SME Support
item.description: ASEAN economic ministers are set to focus on strengthening the region’s digital economy and cross-border digital payments in their upcoming meetings. A key topic is the ASEAN Digital Economy Framework...

item.title: Kenya Advocates Strong International Cooperation to Tackle Rising Drug Challenges
item.description: During the 69th Session of the Commission on Narcotic Drugs in Vienna, Kenya urged the global community to adopt an urgent yet balanced approach to tackle the growing drug menace. Bishop Stephen Mairo...

item.title: Government Supports Women Entrepreneurs in Major Economic Sectors
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Tanzanian government is strongly supporting women entrepreneurs to play bigger roles in the country's economic growth. Speaking at the Women in Business Gala, Minister for Industry...

item.title: Duterte faces potentially long ICC detention if trial proceeds
item.description: One year after former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte was detained at the International Criminal Court (ICC) in The Hague, his 365 days in custody represents only a small fraction of the time oth...

item.title: Joe Rogan questions Trump’s Iran strikes and campaign promises on war
item.description: Joe Rogan, host of a widely followed podcast, openly criticized President Donald Trump’s recent military strikes in Iran, calling the actions confusing and “insane.” During a conversation with author ...

item.title: Ruto Denies Loss of KSh 50 Billion Under Social Health Authority
item.description: President William Ruto has strongly refuted claims that KSh 50 billion was lost under the Social Health Authority (SHA), calling the reports misleading and politically motivated. The controversy follo...

item.title: Chinese Company Publishes AI-Processed Satellite Images of U.S. Military Deployments in the Middle East
item.description: The Chinese company MizarVision, specializing in artificial intelligence, has been publishing high-resolution satellite imagery of U.S. military bases, carrier strike groups, and missile defense insta...
